points: An ecosystem is a functional system of interacting biotic (living) and abiotic (non-living) components.
Organisms are classified as producers (food makers), consumers (food users), and decomposers (waste recyclers).
Natural ecosystems include forests and ponds, while gardens and aquariums are artificial ecosystems.
The $10\%$ energy transfer rule limits food chains to 3 or 4 levels, as energy diminishes progressively.
Energy flow is unidirectional—moving from sunlight to autotrophs to heterotrophs—and does not return to the source.
Food webs are complex networks of branching lines representing multiple feeding relationships in nature.
Biological magnification leads to the concentration of non-degradable chemicals (like pesticides) at higher trophic levels.
The ozone layer ($O_3$) shields life from harmful UV radiation but is depleted by synthetic chemicals like CFCs.
Waste is categorized into biodegradable (organic) and non-biodegradable (persistent), with disposal requiring careful management to prevent environmental damage.
